title: Microlepis oleifolia
taxonomy: Microlepis oleifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Microlepis is part of the tribe Phaseoleae. Further, the classification can be broken down to kingdom Plantae, followed by class Magnoliopsida, and order Fabales.
uses: Microlepis oleifolia is known to have various medicinal properties, although specific information on its uses is limited.  Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat a range of health issues.  Its leaves and roots are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation.  In some cultures, it is also used as a treatment for fever and digestive problems.  Further research is required to confirm the full extent of Microlepis oleifolia's medicinal uses.

The plant is known to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and infections.
